summary: The available literature shows no specific findings about mortality reductions from growth hormone replacement therapy; thus, while treatment improves other health markers, definitive evidence supporting a decrease in all-cause mortality remains lacking. This absence leads to concluding that it may help but lacks proven impact on reducing deaths.
